beach.lineNext: You pointed to a sign, but in dreams, you can't read
stanza: I was dreaming again, you were there with me
We were on the beach, running in the sea
You pointed to a sign, but in dreams, you can't read
And in that moment, I snapped again

beach.linePrev: You've been dead for one month and three days, and we are sleeping in the forest
beach.line: There is sand still in the blankets from the beach where we released you from t…
beach.lineNext: When we wake up, all the clothes that we left out are cold and damp just from t…
stanza: The second time I went to Haida Gwaii was just me and our daughter
Only one month after you died, my face was still contorted
Driving up and down, boots wet inside, aimless and weeping
I needed to return to the place where we discovered that childless, we could bl…
But when we came home, you were pregnant
And then our life together was not long
You had cancer and you were killed and I'm left living like this
Crying on the logging roads with your ashes in a jar
Thinking about the things I'll tell you
When you get back from wherever it is that you've gone
But then I remember death is real
And I'm still here in Masset, it's August 12th, 2016
You've been dead for one month and three days, and we are sleeping in the forest
There is sand still in the blankets from the beach where we released you from t…
When we wake up, all the clothes that we left out are cold and damp just from t…
The ground opens up
Surrounded by growth
Nurse logs with layers of moss and life, young cedars, the sound of water, thic…
The ground absorbs and remakes whatever falls
Nothing dies here
But here is where I came to grieve, to dive into it with you, with your absence
But I keep picking you berries

beach.line: I miss Long Beach and I miss you, babe
beach.lineNext: I miss dancing with you the most of all
stanza: I miss Long Beach and I miss you, babe
I miss dancing with you the most of all
I miss the bar where the Beach Boys would go
Dennis' last stop before Kokomo

beach.linePrev: She went around without a warning, put me in the wall
beach.line: I drove at Long Beach, California with three cracked vertebrae
beach.lineNext: And we went on to Indianapolis, Indiana in May
stanza: After 2000 came 2001
To be the new champions, we were there for to run
From springtime in Arizona, till the fall in Monterey
And the raceways were the battlefields and we fought 'em all the way
Was at Phoenix in the morning, I had a wake-up call
She went around without a warning, put me in the wall
I drove at Long Beach, California with three cracked vertebrae
And we went on to Indianapolis, Indiana in May

beach.linePrev: I fell in with some drifters
beach.line: Cast upon a beach town
beach.lineNext: Winn Dixie cold cuts and highway hand me downs
stanza: I fell in with some drifters
Cast upon a beach town
Winn Dixie cold cuts and highway hand me downs
And I wound up fixing dinner
For them and Boston Jim
I well up with affection
Thinking back down the roads to then
The nets were overflowing
In the Gulf of Mexico
They were overflowing in the refuge of the roads

beach.linePrev: From the bloody cross on top of Calvary
beach.line: To the beach of Malibu
beach.lineNext: Everybody knows it's coming apart
stanza: And everybody knows that you're in trouble
Everybody knows what you've been through
From the bloody cross on top of Calvary
To the beach of Malibu
Everybody knows it's coming apart
Take one last look at this Sacred Heart
Before it blows
And everybody knows

beach.line: Here to Long Beach to Newport by your side

With my bar-t-t-tender, hold me all night
Bar-t-t-tender, keep love alive
Bar-t-t-tender

But the little flower of which I'll tell
Known as the brave acony bell

partner.line: But the little flower of which I'll tell

bell.linePrev: I've made more mistakes than that just tonight
bell.line: So all of you folks in heaven not too a busy ringing the bells
bell.lineNext: Some of us down here aren't doing very well
stanza: Now count every rhododrendon in this cool mountain light
I've made more mistakes than that just tonight
So all of you folks in heaven not too a busy ringing the bells
Some of us down here aren't doing very well
Some of us with our windows open in the southern cross hotel

partner.line: Some of us down here aren't doing very well

bell.linePrev: What made my dreams so hollow was standing at the depot
bell.line: With a steeple full of swallows that could never ring the bell
bell.lineNext: Oh I've come ten thousand miles away, not one thing to show
stanza: What made my dreams so hollow was standing at the depot
With a steeple full of swallows that could never ring the bell
Oh I've come ten thousand miles away, not one thing to show
It was a train that took me away from here
But a train can't bring me home

bell.linePrev: Someone's knockin' at the door
bell.line: Somebody ringin' the bell
bell.lineNext: Someone's knockin' at the door
stanza: Someone's knockin' at the door
Somebody ringin' the bell
Someone's knockin' at the door
Somebody ringin' the bell
Do me a favor
Open the door, and let 'em in, let 'em in now

bell.linePrev: Someone's knockin' at the door
bell.line: Somebody's ringin' the bell
bell.lineNext: Do me a flavor
stanza: Oh, someone's knockin' at the door
Somebody ringin' the bell
Someone's knockin' at the door
Somebody's ringin' the bell
Do me a flavor
Open the door, and let 'em in, yeah, yeah, now
Oh, let 'em in

bell.linePrev: Then smells her perfume in his eyes
bell.line: And her voice was like a bell
bell.lineNext: Outside the streets were steaming
stanza: Romeo Rodriguez squares
His shoulders and curses Jesus
Runs a comb through his black pony-tail
He's thinking of his lonely room
The sink that by his bed gives off a stink
Then smells her perfume in his eyes
And her voice was like a bell

bell.linePrev: To clean the mess that he has dropped
bell.line: Into the life of lithesome Juliette Bell
bell.lineNext: And Romeo wanted Juliette
stanza: Caught between the twisted stars
The plotted lines the faulty map
That brought Columbus to New York
Betwixt between the East and West
He calls on her wearing a leather vest
The earth squeals and shudders to a halt
A diamond crucifix in his ear
Is used to help ward off the fear
That he has left his soul in someone's rented car
Inside his pants he hides a mop
To clean the mess that he has dropped
Into the life of lithesome Juliette Bell

bell.linePrev: With the windows wide by my side
bell.line: And the clusters fell like an empty bell
bell.lineNext: Meteor shower at the motel
stanza: And the clusters fell like an empty bell
Meteor shower at the motel
Where the empty space is a saving grace
Making good time and doing well
Still the question sings like Saturn’s rings
Maybe she knows and she won’t tell

partner.line: Meteor shower at the motel

bell.linePrev: Hold on Magnolia
bell.line: I hear that station bell ring
bell.lineNext: You might be holding the last light I see
stanza: Hold on Magnolia
I hear that station bell ring
You might be holding the last light I see
Before the dark finally gets a hold of me

bell.linePrev: Do you wait for me there?
bell.line: There's a bell in my ears
bell.lineNext: There's the wide, white roar
stanza: There's a bell in my ears
There's the wide, white roar
Drop a bell down the stairs
Hear it fall forevermore
Hear it fall forevermore

bell.linePrev: There's the wide, white roar
bell.line: Drop a bell down the stairs
bell.lineNext: Hear it fall forevermore
stanza: There's a bell in my ears
There's the wide, white roar
Drop a bell down the stairs
Hear it fall forevermore
Hear it fall forevermore

bell.linePrev: Hear it fall forevermore
bell.line: Drop a bell off of the dock
bell.lineNext: Blot it out in the sea
stanza: Drop a bell off of the dock
Blot it out in the sea
Drowning mute as a rock;
And sounding mutiny

bell.linePrev: Our thoughts strayed constantly and without boundary
bell.line: The ringing of the division bell had begun
bell.lineNext: Along the Long Road and on down the Causeway
stanza: Beyond the horizon of the place we lived when we were young
In a world of magnets and miracles
Our thoughts strayed constantly and without boundary
The ringing of the division bell had begun
Along the Long Road and on down the Causeway
Do they still meet there by the Cut
There was a ragged band that followed in our footsteps
Running before time took our dreams away
Leaving the myriad small creatures trying to tie us to the ground
To a life consumed by slow decay

bell.linePrev: They sent me back an Apple phone
bell.line: A fine-hair comb and a bell tolled
bell.lineNext: Bum, bum, bum
stanza: No, it ain't no dream, it’s all too real
How long until this river of blood congeals?
Bum, bum, bum
Sent a letter to my congressman
The Ku Klux Klan, from my pierced hands
Bum, bum, bum
They sent me back an Apple phone
A fine-hair comb and a bell tolled
Bum, bum, bum
The phone rang once and the line went dead
All blood runs red, white pups still bred
Bum, bum, bum

bell.linePrev: And we thought of devotion and snow and distant longing in the Himalayan air
bell.line: High and cold, with a bell ringing out
bell.lineNext: Then right before you died, thirteen years later in our house
stanza: Lying on the rocks, waiting for the boat to come pick us up
I read the one book we had with us aloud
With my head on your lap, sinking into you
Tintin in Tibet in French
And we thought of devotion and snow and distant longing in the Himalayan air
High and cold, with a bell ringing out

bell.linePrev: Clean tick-like to his skin
bell.line: The bell sounds in the corner of the ring a fresh contender for his crown
bell.lineNext: Puts out his pale hand to shake before the bout
stanza: Welcome to hell
I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
Welcome to rooftops
I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
Welcome to the starlight room
I’m sorry, I’ll read that again
Welcome to the underworld
And now, for tonight’s main event
The heavyweight
Close swaddled in the cloying folds of fat
For heavyweight
Squats freak fearful on his stool
And through toad lids he squints his vision of the world
The gray parade of waiters and trade is retinue of course
Seconds thirds and fourths
Clean tick-like to his skin
The bell sounds in the corner of the ring a fresh contender for his crown
Puts out his pale hand to shake before the bout
Champion blinks his sticky tongue swallowing greed
With cuprous speed snakes out

bell.line: Lost like a bell that's tipping over
bell.lineNext: With two cracks along both sides
stanza: Lost like a bell that's tipping over
With two cracks along both sides
And I knew the world was over
So I took a look outside
And watched the fires that were reaching
Up to the weather vanes and the tops of trees
And the waiting scene and the Sunday dream
They're all waiting here for me

bell.songTitle: Last Words of a Shooting Star
bell.linePrev: But I know through mine, you were looking in yours
bell.line: And did you know the Liberty Bell is a replica
bell.lineNext: Silently housed in its original walls
stanza: And did you know the Liberty Bell is a replica
Silently housed in its original walls
And while its dreams played music in the night
Quietly, it was told to believe

bell.linePrev: The dove is never free
bell.line: Ring the bells that still can ring
bell.lineNext: Forget your perfect offering
stanza: Ring the bells that still can ring
Forget your perfect offering
There is a crack, a crack in everything
That's how the light gets in

bell.linePrev: It'll all be yesteryear soon
bell.line: Church bells and now I'm awake
bell.lineNext: And I guess it must be some kind of holiday
stanza: Church bells and now I'm awake
And I guess it must be some kind of holiday
I can't seem to join in the celebration
But I'll go to the service and I'll go to pray
And I'll sing the praises of my maker's name
Like I was as good as she made me

bell.linePrev: And the silver in her hair shines in the cold November air
bell.line: You hear the tolling bell and touch the silk in your lapel
bell.lineNext: And as the teardrops rise to meet thе comfort of the band
stanza: Floating down through the clouds
Memories come rushing up to meet me now
But in the space between the heavens
In the corner of some foreign field
I had a dream
I had a dream
Goodbye Max, goodbye ma
After the service, when you're walking slowly to the car
And the silver in her hair shines in the cold November air
You hear the tolling bell and touch the silk in your lapel
And as the teardrops rise to meet thе comfort of the band
You take her frail hand
And hold on to thе dream

baby's.line: You heard my baby's back in town now
stanza: You heard my baby's back in town now
You should come, come over
We'll be hanging around now
You should come, come over

baby's.linePrev: That they knew nothing but fear
baby's.line: And the baby's in his box, he thinks the door is locked
baby's.lineNext: The sea is in a state, the baby learns to wait
stanza: And the baby's in his box, he thinks the door is locked
The sea is in a state, the baby learns to wait
For the heroine, oh oh for the heroine
Locked in his defense, he waits for the heroine

baby's.linePrev: Well, hey, what's it gonna take
stanza: Some things are melting now
Some things are melting now
Well, hey, what's it gonna take
Till my baby's alright?
What's it gonna take
Till my baby's alright?

baby's.line: The baby's still in his comatose state
stanza: Every day's my wedding day
(Go away world only glass)
The baby's still in his comatose state
(Georgie, they're your favourite)
I'll dye my own Easter eggs
(Skiddly-dee I'm in G, yes)
Just don't go yet, just don't go
(Never go, go so fast)
And Beenie lost the sunset but that's okay
(Go away world only glass)
(Maybe she's hiding in a hot dog)
Does Joe bring flowers to Marilyn's grave?
(Georgie, I swear they're your favourite)
(Got a pig hiding in a truffle)
And girls that eat pizza and never gain weight
(There she goes, there she goes)
(Wearing those purple garters)
Never gain weight, never gain weight
(There she goes home)
(And girl I got a condo in Hoboken)

bowl.linePrev: Then I get afraid of what that could bring
bowl.line: I got a bowling ball in my stomach
bowl.lineNext: I got a desert in my mouth
stanza: Every finger in the room
Is pointing at me
I wanna spit in their faces
Then I get afraid of what that could bring
I got a bowling ball in my stomach
I got a desert in my mouth
Figures that my courage
Would choose to sell out now

bowl.linePrev: And you couldn't see a city
bowl.lineNext: Or a forest or a highway
stanza: In a highway service station
Over the month of June
Was a photograph of the Earth
Taken coming back from the moon
And you couldn't see a city
On that marbled bowling ball
Or a forest or a highway
Or me here least of all
You couldn't see these cold water restrooms
Or this baggage overload
Westbound and rolling, taking refuge in the roads

And swallowtail coats
Tablecloths and patent leather shoes
Bathing suits and bowling balls
And clarinets and rings
And all this radio really needs is a fuse

bowl.linePrev: Seemed like total silence was the only friend I had
bowl.line: A bowl of oatmeal tried to stare me down and won
bowl.lineNext: And it was twelve o'clock before I realized that I was having no fun
stanza: When I woke up this morning, things were looking bad
Seemed like total silence was the only friend I had
A bowl of oatmeal tried to stare me down and won
And it was twelve o'clock before I realized that I was having no fun
Ah, but fortunately I have the key to escape reality

bait.line: While the river was twisting and braiding, the bait bobbed

And the string sobbed
As it cut through the hustling breeze

bawl.lineNext: Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
stanza: Awful atoll —
O, incalculable indiscreetness and sorrow!
Bawl bellow:
Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
Toddle and roll;
Teethe an impalpable bit of leather
While yarrow, heather and hollyhock
Awkwardly molt along the shore

bellow.lineNext: Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
stanza: Awful atoll —
O, incalculable indiscreetness and sorrow!
Bawl bellow:
Sibyl sea-cow, all done up in a bow
Toddle and roll;
Teethe an impalpable bit of leather
While yarrow, heather and hollyhock
Awkwardly molt along the shore

partner.line: Teethe an impalpable bit of leather

blissful.line: Life is thundering blissful towards death
stanza: Life is thundering blissful towards death
Then down and down
And down and down
And down and deeper
Stoke, without sound
The blameless flames
You endless sleeper

bough.linePrev: Till up grows a fine young cherry tree
bough.line: When the bough breaks, what’ll you make for me?
bough.lineNext: A little willow cabin to rest on your knee
stanza: All my bones, they are gone, gone, gone
Take my bones, I don’t need none
Cold, cold cupboard, lord, nothing to chew on!
Suck all day on a cherry stone
Dig a little hole not three inches round —
Spit your pit in a hole in the ground
Weep upon the spot for the starving of me!
Till up grows a fine young cherry tree
When the bough breaks, what’ll you make for me?
A little willow cabin to rest on your knee
What’ll I do with a trinket such as this?
Think of your woman, who’s gone to the west
But I’m starving and freezing in my measly old bed!
Then I’ll crawl across the salt flats, to stroke your sweet head
Come across the desert with no shoes on!
I love you truly
Or I love no-one

bough.linePrev: While outside, the wild boars root
bough.lineNext: O it breaks my heart; I don't know how they do it
stanza: And all at once it came to me
And I wrote and hunched 'till four-thirty
But that vestal light
It burns out with the night
In spite of all the time that we spend on it:
On one bedraggled ghost of a sonnet!
While outside, the wild boars root
Without bending a bough underfoot —
O it breaks my heart; I don't know how they do it
So don't ask me!

barrenly.linePrev: Beneath the sheeting banks of air
barrenly.lineNext: When I could speak, it was too late
stanza: In this life, who did you love
Beneath the drifting ashes
Beneath the sheeting banks of air
That barrenly bore our rations?

barley.linePrev: Didn't you see my heart leap
barley.line: Like a pup in the constant barley?
barley.lineNext: In this new life, where did you crouch
stanza: When I could speak, it was too late
Didn't you hear me calling?
Didn't you see my heart leap
Like a pup in the constant barley?

brakeless.linePrev: While the stars skid away below
brakeless.lineNext: Falling silent as gavels in the snow
stanza: Hung from the underbelly of the earth
While the stars skid away below
Gormless and brakeless, gravel-loose
Falling silent as gavels in the snow

boatload.line: We came by the boatload and were immobilized
boatload.lineNext: Worshiping volcanoes, charting the loping skies
stanza: We came by the boatload and were immobilized
Worshiping volcanoes, charting the loping skies
The tides of the earth left us bound and calcified
And made as obstinate as obsidian, unmoving, save our eyes
Just mooning and blinking from faces marked with coal
Ash cooling and shrinking, cracks loud as thunder rolling
I swear I know you, you know me, where have we met before?
Tell me true, to whose authority do you consign your soul?

birdbath.linePrev: Soaking and felling everything in its path
birdbath.line: And welling in my heart like a birdbath
birdbath.lineNext: It is too short, the day we are born
stanza: I had a dream you came to me
Said, "You shall not do me harm anymore"
And with your knife, you evicted my life
From its little lighthouse on the seashore
And I saw that my blood had no bounds
Spreading in a circle like an atom bomb
Soaking and felling everything in its path
And welling in my heart like a birdbath

blackguard.linePrev: Heard the cup drop, thought, "Well, that's why they keep them around"
blackguard.line: The blackguard sat hard down with no head on him now
blackguard.lineNext: And I felt so bad, 'cause I didn't know how
stanza: From the courtyard, I floated in and watched it go down
Heard the cup drop, thought, "Well, that's why they keep them around"
The blackguard sat hard down with no head on him now
And I felt so bad, 'cause I didn't know how
To feel bad enough to make him proud

blight.linePrev: Shake her skirts and scatter there
blight.line: A shrieking, six-legged millionaire with a blight in his sockets
blight.lineNext: Miss Montez, the Countess of Lansfeld
stanza: Here's Lola — ta da! — to do her famous Spider Dance for you
Lighten up your pockets
Shake her skirts and scatter there
A shrieking, six-legged millionaire with a blight in his sockets

bavaria.linePrev: Miss Montez, the Countess of Lansfeld
bavaria.line: Appealed to the King of Bavaria
bavaria.lineNext: Saying, "Pretty papa, if you are my friend
stanza: Miss Montez, the Countess of Lansfeld
Appealed to the King of Bavaria
Saying, "Pretty papa, if you are my friend
Mister Daddy Longlegs, they are at it again
Can I see you?"

partner.line: Saying, "Pretty papa, if you are my friend

bouquet.linePrev: Ask and you will receive," it is said
bouquet.line: I threw my bouquet and I knocked 'em dead
bouquet.lineNext: Bottle of white, bottle of red
stanza: You asked my hand, hired a band
"In your heart is all that you need
Ask and you will receive," it is said
I threw my bouquet and I knocked 'em dead
Bottle of white, bottle of red
Helpless as a child when you held me in your arms
And I knew that no other could ever love me as you loved
Love me as you loved, but help me, I'm leaving

buoy.linePrev: Sitting up in the star-smoking air
buoy.lineNext: Don't you worry for me, will you have one on me!
stanza: It was dark, I was drunk and half-dead
And we slept, knocking heads
Sitting up in the star-smoking air
Knocking heads like buoys
Don't you worry for me, will you have one on me!
The sight of bridges and balloons
Makes calm canaries irritable;
They caw and claw all afternoon:
"Catenaries and dirigibles
Brace and buoy the living-room—
A loom of metal, warp—woof—wimble"
And a thimble's worth of milky moon
Can touch hearts larger than a thimble

bump.line: Like a bump on a bump on a log, baby;
bump.lineNext: Like I'm in a fistfight with the fog, baby;
stanza: And the tilt of this strange nation
And the will to remain for the duration
(waving the flag
Feeling it drag)
Like a bump on a bump on a log, baby;
Like I'm in a fistfight with the fog, baby;
Step, ball-change, and a pirouette!

bump.linePrev: Anger and no one can heal it
bump.line: Shoves a little bump into the momentum
bump.lineNext: It's just a little lump but you feel it
stanza: Moves like a fist through the traffic
Anger and no one can heal it
Shoves a little bump into the momentum
It's just a little lump but you feel it
In the creases and the shadows
With a rattling deep emotion
The cool, cool river
Sweeps the wild, white ocean

bump.linePrev: Ten feet of water at the bottom of the lake
bump.line: I imagined trout bumping against me in the low diminished light
bump.lineNext: Holding my breath trying to be a boulder
stanza: At night I sit and picture myself curled up beneath
Ten feet of water at the bottom of the lake
I imagined trout bumping against me in the low diminished light
Holding my breath trying to be a boulder
Eroding, to join you in re-mingling with a background
Of churned muck coalescing in the dark
But to get ground back down to matter only
Eternal and dumb becoming not a thing
Abdicating form

bulletproof.linePrev: Do you remember staring up at the stars
bulletproof.line: So far away in their bulletproof cars?
bulletproof.lineNext: When we heard the rushing, slow intake
stanza: Do you remember staring up at the stars
So far away in their bulletproof cars?
When we heard the rushing, slow intake
Of the dark, dark water and the engine breaks

blacksmith.linePrev: A little baby fussing over my legs
blacksmith.line: There is a blacksmith and there is a shepherd
stanza: We take a walk along the dirty lake
Hear the goose cussing at me over her eggs
You poor little cousin, I don't want your dregs
A little baby fussing over my legs
There is a blacksmith and there is a shepherd
And there is a butcher-boy
And there is a barber who's cutting and cutting away
At my only joy
I saw a rabbit as slick as a knife
And as pale as a candlestick
And I had thought it'd be harder to do
But I caught her, and skinned her quick
Held her there, kicking and mewling
Upended, unspooling, unsung and blue
Told her "Wherever you go, little runaway bunny, I will find you"
And then she ran, as they're liable to do

barber.linePrev: Of going to bed with every dream that dies here every morning
barber.line: And so drill me a hole with a barber pole
barber.lineNext: And I'm jumping my parole just like a fugitive tonight
stanza: "Presley's what I go by
Why don't you change the stations?
Count the grain elevators in the rearview mirror"
She said, "Mister, anywhere you point this thing
It's got to beat the hell out of the sting
Of going to bed with every dream that dies here every morning
And so drill me a hole with a barber pole
And I'm jumping my parole just like a fugitive tonight
Why don't you have another swig and pass that car if you're so brave?
I wanna get there before the sun comes up in Burma-Shave
69 ContributorsSapokanikan LyricsShorter than most of the songs on her last alb…
The map of Sapokanikan
Is sanded and bevelled
The land lone and levelled
By some unrecorded and powerful hand

partner.line: The land lone and levelled

backslide.linePrev: The twinned and cast-off shells reveal a single heart of white
backslide.lineNext: Ancient border, sink past the West
stanza: And in an infinite backslide:
Ancient border, sink past the West
Like a sword at the bearer's fall
I can't claim that I knew you best
But did you know me at all?

bearer's.line: Like a sword at the bearer's fall
bearer's.lineNext: I can't claim that I knew you best
stanza: And in an infinite backslide:
Ancient border, sink past the West
Like a sword at the bearer's fall
I can't claim that I knew you best
But did you know me at all?

bedraggle.linePrev: In spite of all the time that we spend on it:
bedraggle.line: On one bedraggled ghost of a sonnet!
bedraggle.lineNext: While outside, the wild boars root
stanza: And all at once it came to me
And I wrote and hunched 'till four-thirty
But that vestal light
It burns out with the night
In spite of all the time that we spend on it:
On one bedraggled ghost of a sonnet!
While outside, the wild boars root
Without bending a bough underfoot —
O it breaks my heart; I don't know how they do it
So don't ask me!

boar.linePrev: On one bedraggled ghost of a sonnet!
boar.line: While outside, the wild boars root
stanza: And all at once it came to me
And I wrote and hunched 'till four-thirty
But that vestal light
It burns out with the night
In spite of all the time that we spend on it:
On one bedraggled ghost of a sonnet!
While outside, the wild boars root
Without bending a bough underfoot —
O it breaks my heart; I don't know how they do it
So don't ask me!

brine.linePrev: So I reach for her hand and her eyes turn to poison
brine.line: And her hair turns to splinters and her flesh turns to brine
brine.lineNext: She leaps across the room, she stands in the window
stanza: So I reach for her hand and her eyes turn to poison
And her hair turns to splinters and her flesh turns to brine
She leaps across the room, she stands in the window
Screams that my first-born will surely be blind
Sing a-to Lorelei-oh

partner.line: Screams that my first-born will surely be blind

stanza: And the mealy worms
In the brine will burn
In a salty pyre
Among the fawns and ferns

bearing.linePrev: With four fine masts and lateen sails
stanza: We sailed away on a winter's day
With fate as malleable as clay
But ships are fallible, I say
And the nautical, like all things, fades
And I can recall our caravel:
A little wicker beetle shell
With four fine masts and lateen sails
Its bearings on Cair Paravel

brace.linePrev: And I'll tie 'em to your shoulders and your feet
brace.line: I'll steal a hacksaw from my dad and cut the braces off your legs
brace.lineNext: And we'll bury them tonight out in the cornfield
stanza: Then we'll spit on Ronnie Arnold and flip him the bird
And slash the tires on the school bus, now don't say a word
I'll take a rusty nail and scratch your initials in my arm
And I'll show you how to sneak up on the roof of the drugstore
I'll take the spokes from your wheelchair and a magpie's wings
And I'll tie 'em to your shoulders and your feet
I'll steal a hacksaw from my dad and cut the braces off your legs
And we'll bury them tonight out in the cornfield
Just put a church key in your pocket, we'll hop that freight train in the hall
We'll slide all the way down the drain to New Orleans in the fall
The sight of bridges and balloons
Makes calm canaries irritable;
They caw and claw all afternoon:
"Catenaries and dirigibles
Brace and buoy the living-room—
A loom of metal, warp—woof—wimble"
And a thimble's worth of milky moon
Can touch hearts larger than a thimble

burnin.line: Let's follow that fire truck, I think your house is burnin' down
burnin.lineNext: And go down to the hobo jungle and kill some rattlesnakes with a trowel
stanza: Let's follow that fire truck, I think your house is burnin' down
And go down to the hobo jungle and kill some rattlesnakes with a trowel
And we'll break all the windows in the old Anderson place
We'll steal a bunch of boysenberries and I'll smear 'em on your face
I'll get a dollar from my mama's purse and buy that skull and crossbones ring
And you can wear it round your neck on an old piece of string

belle.linePrev: I pray for her who prays for me
belle.line: A safe return to my belle France
belle.lineNext: We prayed these wars would end all wars
stanza: I pray for her who prays for me
A safe return to my belle France
We prayed these wars would end all wars
In war, we know, is no romance
And I pray our child will never see
A little Corporal again
Point toward a foreign shore
Captivate the hearts of men

belle.linePrev: Well we were in at the kill again on the Milwaukee Mile
belle.line: And in June up in Michigan we were robbed at Belle Isle
belle.lineNext: Then it was on to Portland, Oregon for the G. I. Joe
stanza: Well we were in at the kill again on the Milwaukee Mile
And in June up in Michigan we were robbed at Belle Isle
Then it was on to Portland, Oregon for the G. I. Joe
And I'd blown off almost everyone when my motor let go
New England, Ontario we died in the dirt
Those walls from mid-Ohio to Toronto, they hurt
So we came to Road America where we burned up at the lake
But at the speedway at Nazareth I made no mistake

stanza: I won't forget the late September
Where we basked among the midnight embers
But it's going like a half-remembered dream
Left and north of where we met
Thinking better of going west
Yeah we lost each other in that river stream
I thought I'd come and find you there
Wait until you lost your hair
And got your place out in the woods
You're not afraid of trees or bears
Or anything with tendencies
To wanna hurt you good

bald.linePrev: With your clothes on the floor
bald.line: Taking advice from some old balding bore
bald.lineNext: You'll ask yourself, "Did I want this at all?"
stanza: Though they may want you to tread in their trail
Only to see if the path they set fails
Though they may want you to take off your clothes
Whatever they think that the action exposed
With your clothes on the floor
Taking advice from some old balding bore
You'll ask yourself, "Did I want this at all?"

bitterness.linePrev: Look down to my aloe cup and take myself a taste
bitterness.line: Bitterness is thick like blood and cold as a wind sea breeze
bitterness.lineNext: If you must drink of me, take of me what you please
stanza: When you ask to drink of me, I think out on the case
Look down to my aloe cup and take myself a taste
Bitterness is thick like blood and cold as a wind sea breeze
If you must drink of me, take of me what you please
I am loathe to say it's the devil's taste
I've been with the devil in the devil's resting place

ballerina.linePrev: But your friendly nose, hangs around
ballerina.line: You're still a ballerina, everybody knows
ballerina.lineNext: But your feet are on the ground
stanza: Zena, you've squared your toes
But your friendly nose, hangs around
You're still a ballerina, everybody knows
But your feet are on the ground
But as the years go by and points comply
With ever more relief
Then patterns in repeat can begin

bon.line: Still, you don't expect to be bright and bon vivant

And many times confused
Yes, and I've often felt forsaken
And certainly misused
Oh, but I'm all right, I'm all right
I'm just weary to my bones
Still, you don't expect to be bright and bon vivant
So far away from home
So far away from home

baffle.linePrev: The minor fall, the major lift
stanza: Now I've heard there was a secret chord
That David played and it pleased the Lord
But you don't really care for music, do ya?
It goes like this, the fourth, the fifth
The minor fall, the major lift
The baffled king composing "Hallelujah"

bathe.linePrev: Your faith was strong, but you needed proof
bathe.line: You saw her bathing on the roof
bathe.lineNext: Her beauty and the moonlight overthrew ya
stanza: Your faith was strong, but you needed proof
You saw her bathing on the roof
Her beauty and the moonlight overthrew ya
She tied you to a kitchen chair
She broke your throne and she cut your hair
And from your lips she drew the Hallelujah

bathe.linePrev: Seal the river at its mouth, take the water prisoner
bathe.line: Fill the sky with screams and cries, bathe in fiery answers
bathe.lineNext: Jesus was an only son and love, his only concept
stanza: Salvation sat and crossed herself and called the Devil "partner"
Wisdom burned upon a shelf, who'll kill the raging cancer
Seal the river at its mouth, take the water prisoner
Fill the sky with screams and cries, bathe in fiery answers
This is a waltz
Thinking about our bodies
What they mean
For our salvation
With only the clothes
That we stand up in
Just the ground
On which we stand
Is the darkness
Ours to take?
Bathed in lightness
Bathed in heat
